enhanced_brief:
PayHub Settlement is an initiative to build a centralised, multi-rail payment and settlement platform for AIA Singapore's insurance operations. Today, premium collections, agent commission disbursements, and customer refund payouts are processed through disconnected systems and manual reconciliation workflows — finance teams rely on spreadsheets and batch file exchanges, resulting in settlement delays of 3–5 business days and a high exception rate that requires significant manual intervention.

The initiative is driven by two converging pressures: upcoming MAS regulations on payment infrastructure resilience, and the operational cost of maintaining fragmented processes across domestic ACH and SEPA-equivalent rails. The owning business unit is Insurance Operations, and the primary users are insurance agents (who need timely commission visibility), finance teams (who manage reconciliation and exception handling), and end customers (who expect prompt refund payouts).

If successful, PayHub Settlement will reduce the average settlement cycle from 3–5 days to same-day for domestic rails and T+1 for cross-border, cut reconciliation exceptions by at least 70%, and provide a full audit ledger for regulatory reporting. Key in-scope capabilities include automated multi-rail reconciliation, exception handling with SLA alerting, agent commission disbursement, customer refund processing, and a compliance-ready audit trail. Out of scope for the initial phase: cross-entity consolidation, FX conversion, and customer-facing payment status self-service.

The business assumes SEPA-equivalent domestic rails are available in target markets and that the existing core banking system can expose the necessary settlement APIs. A phased delivery approach is preferred — MVP targets automated reconciliation and exception handling by Q3, with agent commission and refund modules following in Q4.
